exchanges.question: You've mentioned food, gym, stress control throughout our conversations. But let's talk about the full picture - what does a typical day look like for you in terms of health routines? Sleep, exercise, eating patterns - what are your non-negotiables? The things that if you skip, you feel off?
exchanges.response: You're right, health ante just food matrame kadhu, Food kakunda ante, daily 8 hrs sleep untadhi, stress super control lo untadhi because I always maintain a cool brain. Actually I always hum songs. Songs ni hum cheyydam anedhi, brain ki massage cheyyadam lantidhi. I go to gym daily. I feel everyone has to lift weights, It is very important for the body.
telugu_words_detected: health ante just food matrame kadhu

exchanges.question: Humming songs as brain massage - that's a unique framing. And you mentioned stress is 'super control lo.' But everyone breaks sometimes. When stress does break through your threshold - when it actually gets to you - what does that look like? What triggers it, and how do you pull yourself back?
exchanges.response: To be honest, I have bigger thresholds for patience, anger etc. Once in a while, the thresholds will be break. I will then have zero anger management, even then I don't blabber or ramble. I'll spit the harsh truths with my tone being bit loud. After that session, taking a long bath will help me pull myself together. Time heals me.

exchanges.question: Long bath and time - simple but effective. You said you go to gym daily and believe everyone should lift weights. What's your actual gym routine? Do you follow a program, track your lifts, do progressive overload? And what does the gym give you beyond physical results - is it mental, social, or purely functional?
exchanges.response: I do hypertrophy, I train the same muscle twice a week. Not every muscle workout out there helps me, I tried them all, and now I have a fixed set of workouts. Entha aythe workout chesthano, anthe protein thinta. Na aim okate, muscle ni tear cheyyali, protein tho athinkinchali, It doesn't matter how much weight I'm lifting. chakkaga muscle tear cheyyali, grip strength penchukovali, muscles ki shock ivvali.
telugu_words_detected: Entha aythe workout chesthano, anthe protein thinta

exchanges.question: You've got the physical covered - gym, protein, muscle focus. But what about mental health? You work solo on big projects, you're building things that might take years. Do you ever feel lonely doing this? Anxious about whether any of it will work? How do you take care of your mind the way you take care of your body?
exchanges.response: There is no fixed path towards success. Success ainavallu valla story ni vallaki nacchinattu mold chesi janalaki cheptharu. Everyone thinks, if they follow the same path, they can also end up like that. But following the same path doesn't work for anyone. You have to put your mind, goal, focus, and keep on learning while you are doing it. As simple as that. Inorder to achieve something, we need to have a thirst for knowledge. If everything around, you isn't a black box anymore, you will have the confidence to start anything. I've built a quality network for me talk, vibe, have fun. I never felt lonely working solo. Nenu exciting feel aythene aa project start chestha, throught the project lo some boring work undocchu, vaatiki konchem kastapadatha. Rest I enjoy. I never felt anxious about whether any of it will work or not, because of course, it will work. Let's just say I am alone not capable of achieving it. I'll pull some money. Maybe hire some interns from India or somewhere. Vallatho complete cheyinchukunta, work avvakapovadam anedhi na dictionary lone ledhu.
telugu_words_detected: Success ainavallu valla story ni vallaki nacchinattu mold chesi janalaki cheptharu
Nenu exciting feel aythene aa project start chestha
throught the project lo some boring work undocchu
work avvakapovadam anedhi na dictionary lone ledhu

exchanges.question: You've mentioned gym, sleep, stress control, humming songs. You've got a system. But let's talk about the other side - vices. Everyone has something. Coffee addiction, screen time at 2am, smoking in college, stress eating, doom scrolling. What are YOUR vices - past or present? The things you know aren't great for you but you do anyway. And have you actively quit anything, or do you just manage them?
exchanges.response: I do doom scrolling. Not every day exactly, kani porapatuna start chesthe, antha twaraga end avavdhu. enduku chesana ani chiraku ga untadhi. Janala madhyalo unte, antha em undadhu. Kani okkadine unte maatram appudu appudu it happens. I don't smoke, I don't drink. But If I attend any parties, I don't want to be the guy who bums the vibe. So, I consume alcohol, I never really liked it. Interesting thing is 11 glasses of whiskey did not gave me buzz. When I drank for the first time, I have this fear of what will happen, because of that, after I had two glasses, I lost my motor controls but my brain is still active and I have sense on what I'm speaking. So, taruvatha, bhayam poindhi. elago na mind na control lone untadhi ga ane confidence occhindhi, next time nundi, motor controls kuda bagane unnayi.
telugu_words_detected: enduku chesana ani chiraku ga untadhi
Kani okkadine unte maatram appudu appudu
elago na mind na control lone untadhi ga ane confidence occhindhi

exchanges.question: Let's talk about healthcare decisions. You're investing in organic food, planning to stay active for 50 years. But what about doctors, checkups, medical systems? Are you someone who goes for regular preventive checkups, or do you only see a doctor when something's actually wrong? And in the US healthcare system versus India - how do you navigate that? Do you trust doctors easily, or are you the type to research everything yourself before accepting a diagnosis or treatment?
exchanges.response: Right now, I'm 24, I'm not visiting doctors for regular checkups right now. And doctor's diagnosis ni nammutha nenu, I'll always have my set of questions to enquiry the diagnosis, english medicine, antha prefer cheyyanu. Ma culture lo gani, ma tradition lo gani annitiki cures untay, only at rare conditions, i take english medicines. I always prefer natural remedies. jalubu, jwaram ki assalu tablets veyyanu. I'll provide everything my body needs for it to fight the bacteria or germs. Common intake will be hot water with jeera, I eat only soft foods. I don't burden my body to invest it's time and effort to work on the heavy food. I learned a lot regarding how our body makes miracles with fasting in fast like a girl book. It's a great read.
telugu_words_detected: Ma culture lo gani, ma tradition lo gani annitiki cures untay
jalubu, jwaram ki assalu tablets veyyanu

exchanges.question: You've talked about wanting to stay active for 50 years, about investing in health through organic food, gym, fasting knowledge. Let's talk about your relationship with your own body. At 24, you're building muscle, you're conscious about what goes in. But how do you think about aging? Is there a version of yourself at 50, 60, 70 that you can picture physically? Do you have specific goals - like lifting a certain weight at 60, or running, or just 'still mobile'? And on the flip side - is there anything about your body now that you're working to change, or are you at peace with how it is?
exchanges.response: I haven't that about it yet. I just have to utilize this one life completely to the fullest.

exchanges.question: Last one for health. You've shared your philosophy - 8 hours sleep, gym daily, no English medicine unless necessary, body fights its own battles, fasting knowledge, no regular checkups at 24. Now think about Friday. When you're working on scripts at 2am and Friday notices you've been going for 6 hours straight - should Friday say something? Should Friday remind you to eat, to sleep, to take a break? Or would that feel like nagging? What role should Friday play in your health - silent observer, gentle nudge, or stay out of it completely?
exchanges.response: It would be very great if it actually cares about people it is working, collaborating with. Whoever I recruit in the future would also be using friday to refine the script, collaroborate on new ideas. It should be a friend for all of them. I also want to involve functionality where it can remember people.
